QUESTION: Which top 20 driver created the shortest lap time ever record in a Formula_1 race? Please give them full names.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  shortest lap time refers to MIN(time); the time format for the shortest lap time is 'MM:SS.mmm' or 'M:SS.mmm'; full name of the driver refers to forename, surname

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: List out all the gold medalist winners in cycling sport.
  Hint: gold medalist winners refer to full_name where medal_name = 'Gold'; cycling sport refers to sport_name = 'Cycling';
  SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T5.full_name FROM event AS T1 INNER JOIN competitor_event AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.event_id INNER JOIN games_competitor AS T3 ON T2.competitor_id = T3.id INNER JOIN sport AS T4 ON T1.sport_id = T4.id INNER JOIN person AS T5 ON T3.person_id = T5.id WHERE T4.sport_name = 'Cycling' AND T2.medal_id = 1

Schema:
DATABASE: formula_1

TABLE: circuits
  circuitId (INTEGER) [PK]
  circuitRef (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'sepang', 'bahrain'
  name (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Sepang International Circuit', 'Bahrain International Circuit'
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'Kuala Lumpur', 'Sakhir'
  country (TEXT)  e.g. 'Malaysia', 'Bahrain'
  lat (REAL)  e.g. 2.76083, 26.0325
  lng (REAL)  e.g. 101.738, 50.5106
  alt (INTEGER)
  url (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'http://en.wikipedia.org/wik...', 'http://en.wikipedia.org/wik...'

TABLE: constructorResults
  constructorResultsId (INTEGER) [PK]
  raceId (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 19
  constructorId (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  points (REAL)  e.g. 14.0, 8.0
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'D'
  FK: constructorId → constructors.constructorId
  FK: raceId → races.raceId

TABLE: constructorStandings
  constructorStandingsId (INTEGER) [PK]
  raceId (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 19

  points (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 14.0, 8.0

  positionText (TEXT)  e.g. '1', '3'
  wins (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 0
  FK: constructorId → constructors.constructorId
  FK: raceId → races.raceId

  Q: Please list the full names of all the customers who live in Italy.
  Hint: full name refers to first_name, last_name; Italy refers to country = 'Italy'
  SQL: SELECT T4.first_name, T4.last_name FROM address AS T1 INNER JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.city_id = T2.city_id INNER JOIN country AS T3 ON T2.country_id = T3.country_id INNER JOIN customer AS T4 ON T1.address_id = T4.address_id WHERE T3.country = 'Italy'

DATABASE: formula_1

TABLE: circuits

🤖 model turn:

I’ll inspect the Italy circuits and then verify the fastest lap-time query.
```python
db.execute("SELECT circuitId, name, country FROM circuits WHERE country = 'Italy' ORDER BY name;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT circuitId, name, country FROM circuits WHERE country = 'Italy' ORDER BY name;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT circuitId, name, country FROM circuits WHERE country = 'Italy' ORDER BY name;")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT T1.name,
       (SELECT T4.time
        FROM races AS T2
        INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T4 ON T2.raceId = T4.raceId
        WHERE T2.circuitId = T1.circuitId
        ORDER BY T4.milliseconds ASC
        LIMIT 1) AS lap_record
FROM circuits AS T1
WHERE T1.country = 'Italy'
ORDER BY T1.name;
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT T1.name, (SELECT T4.time FROM races AS T2 INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T4 ON T2.raceId = T4.raceId WHERE T2.circuitId = T1.circuitId ORDER BY T4.milliseconds ASC, T4.time ASC LIMIT 1) AS lap_record FROM circuits AS T1 WHERE T1.country = 'Italy' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM races AS T2 INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T3 ON T2.raceId = T3.raceId WHERE T2.circuitId = T1.circuitId) ORDER BY T1.name;")

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT t.team_long_name, MAX(ta.buildUpPlaySpeed) AS max_speed
FROM Team_Attributes ta
JOIN Team t ON ta.team_api_id = t.team_api_id
GROUP BY ta.team_api_id
ORDER BY max_speed DESC, t.team_long_name
LIMIT 4
""")
```

```python
SELECT t.team_long_name, MAX(ta.buildUpPlaySpeed) AS max_speed
FROM Team_Attributes ta

GROUP BY ta.team_api_id
ORDER BY max_speed DESC, t.team_long_name
LIMIT 4
""")
```

QUESTION: At present, calculate for the player's age who have a sprint speed of no less than 97 between 2013 to 2015.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  players age at present = SUBTRACT((DATETIME(), birthday)); sprint speed of no less than 97 refers to sprint_speed > = 97; between 2013 to 2015 refers to YEAR(date) > = '2013' AND YEAR(date) < = '2015';

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Calculate the average age of the persons who participated in the 1992 Summer Games.
  Hint: DIVIDE(SUM(age), COUNT(person_id)) where games_name = '1992 Summer';
  SQL: SELECT AVG(T2.age) FROM games AS T1 INNER JOIN games_competitor AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.games_id INNER JOIN person AS T3 ON T2.person_id = T3.id WHERE T1.games_name = '1992 Summer'

QUESTION: Who has the highest average finishing rate between the highest and shortest football player?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):

  Q: What are the names of players that have run scored less than 3?
  Hint: scored less than 3 refers to Runs_Scored < 3; name of player refers to Player_name;

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT T1.player_name
FROM Player AS T1
INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id
WHERE T1.height BETWEEN (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player) AND (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player)
GROUP BY T1.player_api_id, T1.player_name
HAVING AVG(T2.finishing) = (
    SELECT MAX(avg_finishing)
    FROM (
        SELECT AVG(T4.finishing) AS avg_finishing
        FROM Player AS T3
        INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS T4 ON T3.player_api_id = T4.player_api_id
        WHERE T3.height BETWEEN (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player) AND (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player)
        GROUP BY T3.player_api_id, T3.player_name
    )
)
""")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT MIN(height), MAX(height) FROM Player")
```
SELECT T1.player_name
FROM Player AS T1
INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2
  ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id
WHERE T1.height = (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player)
   OR T1.height = (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player)
GROUP BY T1.player_api_id, T1.player_name
HAVING AVG(T2.finishing) = (
    SELECT MAX(avg_finishing)
    FROM (
        SELECT AVG(T4.finishing) AS avg_finishing
        FROM Player AS T3
        INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS T4
          ON T3.player_api_id = T4.player_api_id
        WHERE T3.height = (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player)
           OR T3.height = (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player)
        GROUP BY T3.player_api_id, T3.player_name
    )
)

task prompt (10702 chars)
QUESTION: Give the name of the league had the most matches in the 2008/2009 season?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  league that had the most matches in the 2008/2009 season refers to MAX(league_name WHERE season = '2008/2009');

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the name of the team that won the most number of matches in season 1?
  Hint: name of the team refers to Team_Name; the most number of matches refers to max(count(Match_Winner)); season 1 refers to season_Id = 1
  SQL: SELECT Team_Name FROM Team WHERE Team_Id = ( SELECT Match_Winner FROM `Match` WHERE season_Id = 1 GROUP BY Match_Winner ORDER BY COUNT(Match_Winner) DESC LIMIT 1 )

Schema:
DATABASE: european_football_2

TABLE: Country
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium', 'England'

TABLE: League
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium Jupiler League', 'England Premier League'
  FK: country_id → country.id

TABLE: Match
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  league_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  season (TEXT)  e.g. '2008/2009', '2009/2010'
  stage (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 10
  date (TEXT)  e.g. '2008-08-17 00:00:00', '2008-08-16 00:00:00'
  match_api_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 483129, 483130
  home_team_api_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 9987, 10000

task prompt (10806 chars)
QUESTION: How much higher in percentage is Ariel Borysiuk's overall rating than that of Paulin Puel?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  how much higher in percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUBTRACT(overall_rating WHERE player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk', overall_rating WHERE player_name = 'Paulin Puel'), overall_rating WHERE player_name = 'Paulin Puel'), 100);

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the average star with highest percentage for episodes that have received award?
  Hint: received award refers to result = 'Winner'; the highest percentage refers to max(percent); average star = divide(sum(stars), count(episode_id))
  SQL: SELECT T2.person_id FROM Vote AS T1 INNER JOIN Award AS T2 ON T1.episode_id = T2.episode_id ORDER BY T1.percent DESC LIMIT 1

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT p.player_name, pa.overall_rating, pa.date
FROM Player p
JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
WHERE p.player_name IN ('Ariel Borysiuk', 'Paulin Puel')
ORDER BY p.player_name, pa.date
""")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT p.player_name, pa.overall_rating, pa.date FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name IN ('Ariel Borysiuk', 'Paulin Puel') ORDER BY p.player_name, pa.date")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT p.player_name, pa.overall_rating, pa.date FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name IN ('Ariel Borysiuk', 'Paulin Puel') ORDER BY p.player_name, pa.date")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT p.player_name, COUNT(*) AS n, MIN(pa.overall_rating) AS min_rating, MAX(pa.overall_rating) AS max_rating, AVG(pa.overall_rating) AS avg_rating FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name IN ('Ariel Borysiuk', 'Paulin Puel') GROUP BY p.player_name")
```
FINAL("WITH ariel AS (SELECT pa.overall_rating AS rating FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk' ORDER BY pa.date DESC LIMIT 1), paulin AS (SELECT pa.overall_rating AS rating FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' ORDER BY pa.date DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT ((ariel.rating - paulin.rating) * 100.0 / paulin.rating) AS percentage_higher FROM ariel CROSS JOIN paulin;")

FINAL SQL: WITH ariel AS (SELECT pa.overall_rating AS rating FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk' ORDER BY pa.date DESC LIMIT 1), paulin AS (SELECT pa.overall_rating AS rating FROM Player p JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' ORDER BY pa.date DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT ((ariel.rating - paulin.rating) * 100.0 / paulin.rating) AS percentage_higher FROM ariel CROSS JOIN paulin;

PRED result: [[15.254237288135593]]

GOLD SQL: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk' THEN t2.overall_rating ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 - SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' THEN t2.overall_rating ELSE 0 END)) * 100 / SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' THEN t2.overall_rating ELSE 0 END) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id

GOLD result: [[133.7260677466863]]

task prompt (10768 chars)
QUESTION: When was the first time did Kevin Constant have his highest crossing score? Give the date.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Kevin Constant refers to player_name = 'Kevin Constant'; highest crossing score refers to MAX(crossing)

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: For the latest passing player who could play all the positions in the court, how many points did he have in his career?
  Hint: the latest passing refers to max(season_id); play all the positions refers to pos like '%C%' or pos like '%F%' or pos like '%G%'
  SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.points) FROM players AS T1 INNER JOIN players_teams AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T1.pos = 'C-F-G' GROUP BY T2.playerID, T2.year ORDER BY T2.year DESC LIMIT 1

Schema:
DATABASE: european_football_2

TABLE: Country
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium', 'England'

TABLE: League
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium Jupiler League', 'England Premier League'
  FK: country_id → country.id

  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729

task prompt (10649 chars)
QUESTION: What is the difference between players 6 and 23's jumping scores?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  difference between players 6 and 23's jumping scores refers to SUBTRACT(jumping AND id = 6,jumping AND id = 23)

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many players were born in 1982 and have a height above 182cm?
  Hint: born in 1982 refers to birthyear = 1982; height above 182cm refers to height_in_cm > 182 ;
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.ELITEID) FROM height_info AS T1 INNER JOIN PlayerInfo AS T2 ON T1.height_id = T2.height WHERE T1.height_in_cm > 182 AND strftime('%Y', T2.birthdate) = '1982'

Schema:
DATABASE: european_football_2

TABLE: Country
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium', 'England'

TABLE: League
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium Jupiler League', 'England Premier League'
  FK: country_id → country.id

TABLE: Match
  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  league_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  season (TEXT)  e.g. '2008/2009', '2009/2010'
  stage (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 10
  date (TEXT)  e.g. '2008-08-17 00:00:00', '2008-08-16 00:00:00'
  match_api_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 483129, 483130

task prompt (10693 chars)
QUESTION: Please provide top four football players' IDs who are among the lowest potential players and prefer to use the right foot when attacking.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  lowest potential players refers to MIN(potential); prefer to use the right foot when attacking refers to preferred_foot = 'right'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: List all players' given name who are good at both left and right hand and playing the forward position.
  Hint: good at both left and right hand refers to shootCatch IS NULL;  playing the forward position refers to pos = 'F'
  SQL: SELECT nameGiven FROM Master WHERE shootCatch IS NULL AND pos = 'F'

🤖 model turn:

```python
sql = """
SELECT DISTINCT player_api_id
FROM Player_Attributes
WHERE preferred_foot = 'right'
  AND potential = (SELECT MIN(potential) FROM Player_Attributes)
ORDER BY player_api_id
LIMIT 4;
"""
db.execute(sql)
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT DISTINCT player_api_id FROM Player_Attributes WHERE preferred_foot = 'right' AND potential = (SELECT MIN(potential) FROM Player_Attributes) ORDER BY player_api_id LIMIT 4;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT DISTINCT player_api_id FROM Player_Attributes WHERE preferred_foot = 'right' AND potential = (SELECT MIN(potential) FROM Player_Attributes) ORDER BY player_api_id LIMIT 4;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT MIN(potential) AS min_potential FROM Player_Attributes;")
```

task prompt (10581 chars)
QUESTION: Please state the finishing rate and curve score of the player who has the heaviest weight.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  finishing rate refer to finishing; curve score refer to curve; heaviest weight refers to MAX(weight)

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the weight in pounds of the heaviest player?
  Hint: weight in pounds refers to weight_in_lbs; heaviest player refers to MAX(weight_in_lbs);
  SQL: SELECT MAX(T2.weight_in_lbs) FROM PlayerInfo AS T1 INNER JOIN weight_info AS T2 ON T1.weight = T2.weight_id

DATABASE: european_football_2

TABLE: Country
  id (INTEGER) [PK]

  id (INTEGER) [PK]
  country_id (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 1729
  name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Belgium Jupiler League', 'England Premier League'

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence

  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.

task prompt (3846 chars)
QUESTION: For the branch which located in the south Bohemia with biggest number of inhabitants, what is the percentage of the male clients?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Percentage of the male clients = DIVIDE(COUNT(male clients), COUNT(clients)) * 100; Male refers to gender = 'M', A3 is the region name. A4 contains the information about inhabitants.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Among the male customers, how many of them come from a place with over 30,000 inhabitants?
  Hint: SEX = 'Male', over 30,000 inhabitants refer to NHABITANTS_K > 30; place refers to GEOID;
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.GEOID) FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Demog AS T2 ON T1.GEOID = T2.GEOID WHERE T1.SEX = 'Male' AND T2.INHABITANTS_K > 30

Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'
  FK: client_id → client.client_id
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'
  A5 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '80'
  A6 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '26'
  A7 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '6'
  A8 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  A9 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 5
  A10 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 100.0, 46.7
  A11 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 12541, 8507
  A12 (REAL)  e.g. 0.2, 1.6
  A13 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0.43, 1.85
  A14 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 167, 132
  A15 (INTEGER)  e.g. 85677, 2159
  A16 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 99107, 2674

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN gender = 'M' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_of_male_clients
FROM client
WHERE district_id = (
  SELECT district_id
  FROM district
  WHERE A3 = 'south Bohemia'
  ORDER BY CAST(A4 AS INTEGER) DESC
  LIMIT 1
);
""")
```

to=python.db.execute 平台直属_code
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN gender = 'M' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_of_male_clients
FROM client
WHERE district_id = (

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN gender = 'M' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_of_male_clients
FROM client
WHERE district_id = (
  SELECT district_id
  FROM district
  WHERE A3 = 'south Bohemia'
  ORDER BY CAST(A4 AS INTEGER) DESC
  LIMIT 1
);
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT
  100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN c.client_id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS percentage_of_male_clients
FROM client AS c
INNER JOIN disp AS d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
INNER JOIN account AS a ON d.account_id = a.account_id
WHERE a.district_id = (
  SELECT district_id
  FROM district
  WHERE A3 = 'south Bohemia'
  ORDER BY CAST(A4 AS INTEGER) DESC
  LIMIT 1
);
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN c.client_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id), 0) AS percentage_of_male_clients FROM client AS c INNER JOIN disp AS d ON c.client_id = d.client_id INNER JOIN account AS a ON d.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.district_id = ( SELECT district_id FROM district WHERE A3 = 'south Bohemia' ORDER BY CAST(A4 AS INTEGER) DESC LIMIT 1 )")

QUESTION: What are the symptoms observed by the youngest patient to ever did a medical examination? Identify their diagnosis.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa; symptoms observed refers to the symptoms is not NULL

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Among the patients that died, what is the condition of the oldest patient?
  Hint: if deathdate is not null, it means this patient died; condition refers to DESCRIPTION from conditions; the oldest patient refers to MAX(SUBTRACT(strftime('%Y', deathdate), strftime('%Y', birthdate)));

DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

  ID (INTEGER)  e.g. 14872, 48473

I’ll check the relevant rows to make sure the youngest examined patient and their non-NULL symptoms/diagnosis are identified correctly.
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT p.ID, p.Birthday, e.`Examination Date`, e.Symptoms, e.Diagnosis
FROM Patient p
JOIN Examination e ON e.ID = p.ID
WHERE e.Symptoms IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY p.Birthday DESC, e.`Examination Date` DESC
LIMIT 10
""")
```
```python

LIMIT 10
```

task prompt (3365 chars)
QUESTION: The oldest SJS patient's medical laboratory work was completed on what date, and what age was the patient when they initially arrived at the hospital?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa; 'SJS' refers to diagnosis; (SUBTRACT(year(`First Date`)), year(Birthday)); age of the patients when they initially arrived at the hospital refers to year(Birthday)

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Among the patients that died, what is the condition of the oldest patient?
  Hint: if deathdate is not null, it means this patient died; condition refers to DESCRIPTION from conditions; the oldest patient refers to MAX(SUBTRACT(strftime('%Y', deathdate), strftime('%Y', birthdate)));
  SQL: SELECT T1.DESCRIPTION FROM conditions AS T1 INNER JOIN patients AS T2 ON T1.PATIENT = T2.patient WHERE T2.deathdate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY strftime('%Y', T2.deathdate) - strftime('%Y', T2.birthdate) DESC LIMIT 1

FINAL("SELECT L.`Date`, CAST(strftime('%Y', P.`First Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE E.Diagnosis = 'SJS' ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, L.`Date` ASC LIMIT 1")

⚙️ observation:

{'table': 'Examination', 'column': 'Diagnosis', 'values': ['MCTD, AMI', 'SLE', 'PSS', 'SLE, SjS, vertigo', 'SLE, AIHA', 'Raynaud', 'SLE, ITP, LA(-)', 'Behcet, SjS susp', 'SLE, SjS, ITP', 'PSS, SjS, IP'], 'error': None}

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("SELECT P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis, L.Date FROM Patient P JOIN Examination E ON P.ID=E.ID JOIN Laboratory L ON P.ID=L.ID WHERE E.Diagnosis LIKE '%SjS%' OR E.Diagnosis LIKE '%SJS%' ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, L.Date ASC LIMIT 10")
```

FINAL("SELECT L.`Date`, CAST(strftime('%Y', P.`First Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%' ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, L.`Date` ASC LIMIT 1")

⚙️ observation:

Code executed successfully (no output)

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis, COUNT(L.Date) AS lab_count
FROM Patient AS P
JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID
LEFT JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID
WHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%'
GROUP BY P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis
ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC
LIMIT 20
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT L.`Date`, CAST(strftime('%Y', P.`First Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%' ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, L.`Date` ASC LIMIT 1")

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT P.ID, P.SEX, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis, L.Date
FROM Patient AS P
JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID
LEFT JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID
WHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%'
ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, P.ID ASC, L.Date ASC
LIMIT 20
""")
```

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis,
       MIN(L.Date) AS first_lab_date,
       CAST(strftime('%Y', P.`First Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age_at_first_date
FROM Patient AS P
JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID
JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID
WHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%'
GROUP BY P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis
ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, P.ID ASC
LIMIT 10
""")
```

FINAL("SELECT L.`Date`, CAST(strftime('%Y', P.`First Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%' ORDER BY P.Birthday ASC, L.`Date` ASC LIMIT 1")

    "sql": "SELECT P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis, COUNT(L.Date) AS lab_count\nFROM Patient AS P\nJOIN Examination AS E ON P.ID = E.ID\nLEFT JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID\nWHERE UPPER(E.Diagnosis) LIKE '%SJS%'\nGROUP BY P.ID, P.Birthday, P.`First Date`, E.Diagnosis\nORDER BY P.Birthday ASC\nLIMIT 20"

bird_1175 [thrombosis_prediction / moderate] ✘ WRONG — How old was the patient who had the highest hemoglobin count at the time of the examination, and what is the d
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (3206 chars)
QUESTION: How old was the patient who had the highest hemoglobin count at the time of the examination, and what is the doctor's diagnosis?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  How old the patient refers to SUBTRACT(year(`Examination Date`), year(Birthday)); the highest hemoglobin count refers to MAX(HGB)

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: The highest Systolic Blood Pressure was observed in which patient? Please give his or her full name.
  Hint: the highest Systolic Blood Pressure refers to MAX(DESCRIPTION = 'Systolic Blood Pressure') from observations; full name refers to first, last;
  SQL: SELECT T1.first, T1.last FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN observations AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE T2.VALUE = ( SELECT MAX(VALUE) FROM observations WHERE description = 'Systolic Blood Pressure' ) LIMIT 1

Schema:
DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

TABLE: Examination
  ID (INTEGER)  e.g. 14872, 48473
  Examination Date (DATE)  e.g. '1997-05-27', '1992-12-21'
  aCL IgG (REAL)  e.g. 1.3, 4.3
  aCL IgM (REAL)  e.g. 1.6, 4.6
  ANA (INTEGER)  e.g. 256, 0
  ANA Pattern (TEXT)  e.g. 'P', 'P,S'
  aCL IgA (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 3
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'MCTD, AMI', 'SLE'
  KCT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  RVVT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  LAC (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  Symptoms (TEXT)  e.g. 'AMI', 'CNS lupus'
  Thrombosis (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 0
  FK: ID → Patient.ID

TABLE: Laboratory
  ID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (DATE) [PK, NOT NULL]
  GOT (INTEGER)  e.g. 34, 29
  GPT (INTEGER)  e.g. 36, 31
  LDH (INTEGER)  e.g. 567, 579
  ALP (INTEGER)  e.g. 166, 154
  TP (REAL)  e.g. 4.5, 5.1
  ALB (REAL)  e.g. 3.3, 3.4
  UA (REAL)  e.g. 3.8, 4.2
  UN (INTEGER)  e.g. 29, 36
  CRE (REAL)  e.g. 0.8, 0.9
  T-BIL (REAL)  e.g. 0.3, 0.2
  T-CHO (INTEGER)  e.g. 165, 134
  TG (INTEGER)  e.g. 185, 205
  CPK (INTEGER)  e.g. 9, 10
  GLU (INTEGER)  e.g. 88, 134
  WBC (REAL)  e.g. 5.0, 10.4
  RBC (REAL)  e.g. 2.6, 2.9
  HGB (REAL)  e.g. 6.4, 6.7
  HCT (REAL)  e.g. 20.3, 21.6
  PLT (INTEGER)  e.g. 227, 242
  PT (REAL)  e.g. 11.3, 12.0
  APTT (INTEGER)  e.g. 108, 84
  FG (REAL)  e.g. 27.0, 38.6
  PIC (INTEGER)  e.g. 320, 513
  TAT (INTEGER)  e.g. 77, 108
  TAT2 (INTEGER)  e.g. 113, 88
  U-PRO (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '0'
  IGG (INTEGER)  e.g. 339, 771
  IGA (INTEGER)  e.g. 145, 188
  IGM (INTEGER)  e.g. 46, 132
  CRP (TEXT)  e.g. '0.6', '2.7'
  RA (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  RF (TEXT)  e.g. '<20.5', '<20.8'
  C3 (INTEGER)  e.g. 30, 82
  C4 (INTEGER)  e.g. 14, 26
  RNP (TEXT)  e.g. '0', '1'
  SM (TEXT)  e.g. '0', '1'
  SC170 (TEXT)  e.g. '16', '1'
  SSA (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  SSB (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  CENTROMEA (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  DNA (TEXT)  e.g. '41.9', '18'
  DNA-II (INTEGER)
  FK: ID → Patient.ID

TABLE: Patient
  ID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  SEX (TEXT)  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  Birthday (DATE)  e.g. '1934-02-13', '1937-05-02'
  Description (DATE)  e.g. '1994-02-14', '1996-12-01'
  First Date (DATE)  e.g. '1993-02-10', '1973-01-01'
  Admission (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'RA susp.', 'PSS'

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT CAST(strftime('%Y', E.`Examination Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age,
       E.Diagnosis
FROM Laboratory AS L
JOIN Examination AS E
  ON L.ID = E.ID
 AND L.Date = E.`Examination Date`
JOIN Patient AS P
  ON P.ID = E.ID
WHERE L.HGB = (SELECT MAX(HGB) FROM Laboratory)
LIMIT 5;
""")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT MAX(HGB) AS max_hgb FROM Laboratory")
```
FINAL("SELECT CAST(strftime('%Y', T2.`Examination Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', T3.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age, T2.Diagnosis FROM Laboratory AS T1 INNER JOIN Examination AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID AND T1.Date = T2.`Examination Date` INNER JOIN Patient AS T3 ON T2.ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.HGB = (SELECT MAX(HGB) FROM Laboratory) LIMIT 1")

  "sequence": 5,
  "turn": 5,
  "tool": "db.execute",
  "arguments": {
    "sql": "SELECT ID, Date, HGB\nFROM Laboratory\nWHERE HGB = (SELECT MAX(HGB) FROM Laboratory)"
  },

FINAL SQL: SELECT CAST(strftime('%Y', E.`Examination Date`) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age, E.Diagnosis FROM Laboratory AS L JOIN Patient AS P ON P.ID = L.ID JOIN Examination AS E ON E.ID = L.ID WHERE L.HGB = (SELECT MAX(HGB) FROM Laboratory) ORDER BY CASE WHEN E.`Examination Date` = L.Date THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, E.`Examination Date` DESC LIMIT 1

bird_1179 [thrombosis_prediction / moderate] ✘ WRONG — For the patient who was diagnosed with SLE on 1994/2/19, what was his/her anti-Cardiolipin antibody concentrat
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):

PROTOCOL:

  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.

task prompt (3296 chars)
QUESTION: For the patient who was diagnosed with SLE on 1994/2/19, what was his/her anti-Cardiolipin antibody concentration status on 1993/11/12?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  diagnosed with SLE refers to Diagnosis = 'SLE'; 1994/2/19 refers to Description = '1994-02-19'; anti-Cardiolipin refers to aCL IgM; 1993/11/12 refers to Examination Date = '1993/11/12'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many immunizations did the patient with the most prevalent condition that started recently get?
  Hint: patient with the most prevalent condition refers to patient where MAX(PREVALENCE RATE); started recently refers to MAX(START);
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.patient) FROM all_prevalences AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON lower(T1.ITEM) = lower(T2.DESCRIPTION) INNER JOIN immunizations AS T3 ON T2.PATIENT = T3.PATIENT GROUP BY T2.PATIENT ORDER BY T2.START DESC, T1."PREVALENCE RATE" DESC LIMIT 1

Schema:
DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

TABLE: Examination
  ID (INTEGER)  e.g. 14872, 48473
  Examination Date (DATE)  e.g. '1997-05-27', '1992-12-21'
  aCL IgG (REAL)  e.g. 1.3, 4.3
  aCL IgM (REAL)  e.g. 1.6, 4.6
  ANA (INTEGER)  e.g. 256, 0
  ANA Pattern (TEXT)  e.g. 'P', 'P,S'
  aCL IgA (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 3
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'MCTD, AMI', 'SLE'
  KCT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  RVVT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  LAC (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  Symptoms (TEXT)  e.g. 'AMI', 'CNS lupus'
  Thrombosis (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 0
  FK: ID → Patient.ID

TABLE: Laboratory
  ID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (DATE) [PK, NOT NULL]
  GOT (INTEGER)  e.g. 34, 29
  GPT (INTEGER)  e.g. 36, 31
  LDH (INTEGER)  e.g. 567, 579
  ALP (INTEGER)  e.g. 166, 154
  TP (REAL)  e.g. 4.5, 5.1
  ALB (REAL)  e.g. 3.3, 3.4
  UA (REAL)  e.g. 3.8, 4.2
  UN (INTEGER)  e.g. 29, 36
  CRE (REAL)  e.g. 0.8, 0.9
  T-BIL (REAL)  e.g. 0.3, 0.2
  T-CHO (INTEGER)  e.g. 165, 134
  TG (INTEGER)  e.g. 185, 205
  CPK (INTEGER)  e.g. 9, 10
  GLU (INTEGER)  e.g. 88, 134
  WBC (REAL)  e.g. 5.0, 10.4
  RBC (REAL)  e.g. 2.6, 2.9
  HGB (REAL)  e.g. 6.4, 6.7
  HCT (REAL)  e.g. 20.3, 21.6
  PLT (INTEGER)  e.g. 227, 242
  PT (REAL)  e.g. 11.3, 12.0
  APTT (INTEGER)  e.g. 108, 84
  FG (REAL)  e.g. 27.0, 38.6
  PIC (INTEGER)  e.g. 320, 513
  TAT (INTEGER)  e.g. 77, 108
  TAT2 (INTEGER)  e.g. 113, 88
  U-PRO (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '0'
  IGG (INTEGER)  e.g. 339, 771
  IGA (INTEGER)  e.g. 145, 188
  IGM (INTEGER)  e.g. 46, 132
  CRP (TEXT)  e.g. '0.6', '2.7'
  RA (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  RF (TEXT)  e.g. '<20.5', '<20.8'
  C3 (INTEGER)  e.g. 30, 82
  C4 (INTEGER)  e.g. 14, 26
  RNP (TEXT)  e.g. '0', '1'
  SM (TEXT)  e.g. '0', '1'
  SC170 (TEXT)  e.g. '16', '1'
  SSA (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  SSB (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  CENTROMEA (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  DNA (TEXT)  e.g. '41.9', '18'
  DNA-II (INTEGER)
  FK: ID → Patient.ID

TABLE: Patient
  ID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  SEX (TEXT)  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  Birthday (DATE)  e.g. '1934-02-13', '1937-05-02'
  Description (DATE)  e.g. '1994-02-14', '1996-12-01'
  First Date (DATE)  e.g. '1993-02-10', '1973-01-01'
  Admission (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'RA susp.', 'PSS'

```python
  ON P.ID = E.ID AND E.`Examination Date` = '1993-11-12'
WHERE P.Diagnosis = 'SLE'
  AND P.Description = '1994-02-19'
```

bird_118 [financial / moderate] ✘ WRONG — For loan amount less than USD100,000, what is the percentage of accounts that is still running with no issue.
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (3913 chars)
QUESTION: For loan amount less than USD100,000, what is the percentage of accounts that is still running with no issue.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Status = 'C' stands for running contract, ok so far; Percentage of accounts by condition = [(total(amount) & condition) / (total amount)] * 100.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Among the customers with an account balance lower than 4000, what is the percentage of the customers in the US?
  Hint: DIVIDE(COUNT(c_custkey where n_name = 'United States' and c_acctbal < 4000), COUNT(c_custkey where c_acctbal < 4000)) as percentage;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T2.n_name = 'United States', 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.c_custkey) FROM customer AS T1 INNER JOIN nation AS T2 ON T1.c_nationkey = T2.n_nationkey WHERE T1.c_acctbal < 4000

Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'
  FK: client_id → client.client_id
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'
  A5 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '80'
  A6 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '26'
  A7 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '6'
  A8 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  A9 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 5
  A10 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 100.0, 46.7
  A11 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 12541, 8507
  A12 (REAL)  e.g. 0.2, 1.6
  A13 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0.43, 1.85
  A14 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 167, 132
  A15 (INTEGER)  e.g. 85677, 2159
  A16 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 99107, 2674

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

task prompt (2975 chars)
QUESTION: Was the patient with the number 57266's uric acid within a normal range?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  uric acid within a normal range refers to UA > 8.0 and SEX = 'M'OR UA > 6.5 and SEX = 'F'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many white patients have the reason code of 10509002?
  Hint: white refers to race = 'white'; reason code of 10509002 refers to careplans.REASONCODE = '10509002';
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.PATIENT) FROM careplans AS T1 INNER JOIN patients AS T2 ON T1.PATIENT = T2.patient WHERE T2.race = 'white' AND T1.REASONCODE = '10509002'

Schema:
DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

TABLE: Examination
  ID (INTEGER)  e.g. 14872, 48473
  Examination Date (DATE)  e.g. '1997-05-27', '1992-12-21'
  aCL IgG (REAL)  e.g. 1.3, 4.3
  aCL IgM (REAL)  e.g. 1.6, 4.6
  ANA (INTEGER)  e.g. 256, 0
  ANA Pattern (TEXT)  e.g. 'P', 'P,S'
  aCL IgA (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 3
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'MCTD, AMI', 'SLE'

task prompt (3331 chars)
QUESTION: List and group all patients by sex for total bilirubin (T-BIL) level not within the normal range.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  List refers to GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ID); total bilirubin (T-BIL) not within normal range refers to T-BIL > = 2.0

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Among the patients who have been using Penicillin V Potassium 250 MG, what percentage of patients are female?
  Hint: Penicillin V Potassium 250 MG refers to medications.DESCRIPTION = 'Penicillin V Potassium 250 MG'; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(patients.patient WHERE gender = 'F'), COUNT(patients.patient) WHERE medications.DESCRIPTION = 'Penicillin V Potassium 250 MG'), 100) female refers to gender = 'F';
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.gender = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.patient) FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN medications AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE T2.DESCRIPTION = 'Penicillin V Potassium 250 MG'

task prompt (3187 chars)
QUESTION: Name the ID and age of patient with two or more laboratory examinations which show their hematoclit level exceeded the normal range.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  age = SUBTRACT(year(current_timestamp), year(Birthday)); patient with two or more laboratory examinations refers to COUNT(ID) > 2; hematoclit level exceeded the normal range refers to HCT > = 52;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: List the patient ids whose disease has the most occurrences.
  Hint: patient ids refers to patients.patient; disease with the most occurrence refers to MAX(all_prevalences.OCCURENCES);
  SQL: SELECT T1.patient FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT INNER JOIN all_prevalences AS T3 ON T3.ITEM = T2.DESCRIPTION ORDER BY T3.OCCURRENCES DESC LIMIT 1

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT T1.ID,
       CAST(strftime('%Y', 'now') AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', T1.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age
FROM Patient AS T1
INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID
WHERE T2.HCT >= 52
GROUP BY T1.ID
HAVING COUNT(T2.ID) > 2
""")
```

WHERE T2.HCT >= 52
GROUP BY T1.ID
HAVING COUNT(T2.ID) > 2

task prompt (3215 chars)
QUESTION: For patients with abnormal platelet level, state the number of patients with lower than normal range. How is it compare to the number of patients with higher than normal range?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  abnormal platelet level refers to PLT <= 100 or PLT >= 400; platelet level lower than normal range refers to PLT < 100; calculation = SUBTRACT(SUM(PLT < 100), SUM(PLT > 400)); platelet level higher than normal range refers to PLT > 400;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: State the prevalence rate of condition no. 368581000119106.
  Hint: condition no. 368581000119106 refers to conditions where CODE = '368581000119106';
  SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1."PREVALENCE RATE" FROM all_prevalences AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON lower(T1.ITEM) = lower(T2.DESCRIPTION) WHERE T2.code = '368581000119106'

```python
WHERE PLT <= 100 OR PLT >= 400;
```

system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers

task prompt (3810 chars)
QUESTION: For laboratory examinations take in 1984, list all patients below 50 years old with normal platelet level.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  laboratory examinations take in 1984 refers to YEAR(Date) = '1984'; below 50 years old = SUBTRACT(year(current_timestamp), year(Birthday)) < 50; normal platelet level refers to PLT between 100 and 400;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the percentage of the most common conditions for patients age 60 and above?
  Hint: most common condition refers to MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)); age 60 and above refers to SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)) AND SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60))), COUNT(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)), 10);
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T5.DESCRIPTION = T3.DESCRIPTION THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T3.patient) FROM ( SELECT T2.DESCRIPTION, T1.patient FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T2.START) - strftime('%J', T1.birthdate)) / 365) > 60 GROUP BY T2.DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.DESCRIPTION) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T3 INNER JOIN patients AS T4 ON T3.patient = T4.patient INNER JOIN conditions AS T5 ON T4.patient = T5.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T5.START) - strftime('%J', T4.birthdate)) / 365) > 60

task prompt (3859 chars)
QUESTION: For all patients who are older than 55 years old, what is the percentage of female who has abnormal prothrombin time (PT)?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  older than 55 years old = SUBTRACT(year(current_timestamp), year(Birthday)) > 55; abnormal prothrombin time (PT) refers to PT > = 14; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(PT > = 14 AND SEX = 'F'), SUM(PT > = 14)) * 100; female refers to sex = 'F';

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the percentage of the most common conditions for patients age 60 and above?
  Hint: most common condition refers to MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)); age 60 and above refers to SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)) AND SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60))), COUNT(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)), 10);
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T5.DESCRIPTION = T3.DESCRIPTION THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T3.patient) FROM ( SELECT T2.DESCRIPTION, T1.patient FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T2.START) - strftime('%J', T1.birthdate)) / 365) > 60 GROUP BY T2.DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.DESCRIPTION) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T3 INNER JOIN patients AS T4 ON T3.patient = T4.patient INNER JOIN conditions AS T5 ON T4.patient = T5.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T5.START) - strftime('%J', T4.birthdate)) / 365) > 60

  Description (DATE)  e.g. '1994-02-14', '1996-12-01'

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT
  CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN L.PT >= 14 AND P.SEX = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0
  / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN L.PT >= 14 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS percentage
FROM Patient AS P
JOIN Laboratory AS L
  ON P.ID = L.ID
WHERE (CAST(strftime('%Y', 'now') AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER)) > 55;
""")
```

```python
SELECT
  CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN L.PT >= 14 AND P.SEX = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0
  / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN L.PT >= 14 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS percentage
FROM Patient AS P
JOIN Laboratory AS L
  ON P.ID = L.ID
WHERE (CAST(strftime('%Y', 'now') AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER)) > 55;
""")
```

task prompt (3099 chars)
QUESTION: Among the male patients who have a normal level of white blood cells, how many of them have an abnormal fibrinogen level?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  male patients refers to Sex = 'M'; normal level of white blood cells refers to WBC > 3.5 and WBC <9.0; abnormal fibrinogen level refers to FG < = 150 or FG > = 450; Don't compute repetitive ones.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many male patients have prediabetes condition?
  Hint: male refers to gender = 'M'; prediabetes condition refers to conditions.DESCRIPTION = 'Prediabetes';
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.patient) FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 WHERE T2.DESCRIPTION = 'Prediabetes' AND T1.gender = 'M'

Schema:
DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

TABLE: Examination
  ID (INTEGER)  e.g. 14872, 48473
  Examination Date (DATE)  e.g. '1997-05-27', '1992-12-21'
  aCL IgG (REAL)  e.g. 1.3, 4.3
  aCL IgM (REAL)  e.g. 1.6, 4.6
  ANA (INTEGER)  e.g. 256, 0
  ANA Pattern (TEXT)  e.g. 'P', 'P,S'
  aCL IgA (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 3
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'MCTD, AMI', 'SLE'
  KCT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  RVVT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence

  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.

task prompt (3927 chars)
QUESTION: For loans contracts which are still running where client are in debt, list the district of the and the state the percentage unemployment rate increment from year 1995 to 1996.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Unemployment increment rate in percentage = [(unemployment rate 2016 - unemployment rate 2015) / unemployment rate 2015] * 100; unemployment rate 2015 appears in the A12; unemployment rate 2016 appears in the A13; Loan contracts which are still running where client are in debt can be presented as status = 'D'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Calculate the percentage of customers' accounts in debt.
  Hint: DIVIDE(COUNT(c_custkey where c_acctbal < 0), COUNT(c_custkey)) as percentage;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(c_acctbal < 0, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(c_custkey) FROM customer

Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'
  FK: client_id → client.client_id
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'
  A5 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '80'
  A6 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '26'
  A7 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '6'
  A8 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  A9 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 5
  A10 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 100.0, 46.7
  A11 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 12541, 8507
  A12 (REAL)  e.g. 0.2, 1.6
  A13 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0.43, 1.85
  A14 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 167, 132
  A15 (INTEGER)  e.g. 85677, 2159
  A16 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 99107, 2674

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

task prompt (3090 chars)
QUESTION: How many patients with an Ig G higher than normal?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Ig G higher than normal refers to IGG >= 2000; Should consider DISTINCT in the final result;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the average body weight of Asian patients?
  Hint: average = AVG(observations.VALUE WHERE observations.DESCRIPTION = 'Body Weight' AND observations.UNITS = 'kg'); body weight refers to observations.DESCRIPTION = 'Body Weight'; Asian refers to race = 'asian';
  SQL: SELECT SUM(T2.VALUE) / COUNT(T1.patient) FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN observations AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE T1.race = 'asian' AND T2.DESCRIPTION = 'Body Weight' AND T2.UNITS = 'kg'

task prompt (2956 chars)
QUESTION: Among the patients with a normal Ig G level, how many of them have symptoms?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  normal Ig G level refers to IGG > 900 and IGG < 2000; have symptoms refers to Symptoms IS NOT NULL;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many patients have the most prevalent conditions?
  Hint: the most prevalent conditions refer to MAX(PREVALENCE RATE);
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.patient) FROM all_prevalences AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON lower(T1.ITEM) = lower(T2.DESCRIPTION) ORDER BY T1."PREVALENCE RATE" DESC LIMIT 1

Schema:
DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

TABLE: Examination

```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) AS rows, COUNT(DISTINCT ID) AS patients FROM Examination;")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT L.ID) AS count
FROM Laboratory AS L
WHERE L.IGG > 900 AND L.IGG < 2000
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM Examination AS E
    WHERE E.ID = L.ID
      AND E.Symptoms IS NOT NULL
  );
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT L.ID) AS count FROM Laboratory AS L WHERE L.IGG > 900 AND L.IGG < 2000 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Examination AS E WHERE E.ID = L.ID AND E.Symptoms IS NOT NULL);")

```python
  AND CAST(strftime('%Y', P.`First Date`) AS INTEGER) >= 1990
""")
```

task prompt (3024 chars)
QUESTION: Among the patients with normal anti-SM, how many of them does not have thrombosis?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  normal anti-SM refers to SM IN('-', '+-'); SM = 'negative' means '-'; SM = '0' means '+-'; SM = '1' means '+'; does not have thrombosis refers to Thrombosis = 0;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many patients have the most prevalent conditions?
  Hint: the most prevalent conditions refer to MAX(PREVALENCE RATE);
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.patient) FROM all_prevalences AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON lower(T1.ITEM) = lower(T2.DESCRIPTION) ORDER BY T1."PREVALENCE RATE" DESC LIMIT 1

Schema:
DATABASE: thrombosis_prediction

TABLE: Examination
  ID (INTEGER)  e.g. 14872, 48473
  Examination Date (DATE)  e.g. '1997-05-27', '1992-12-21'
  aCL IgG (REAL)  e.g. 1.3, 4.3
  aCL IgM (REAL)  e.g. 1.6, 4.6
  ANA (INTEGER)  e.g. 256, 0
  ANA Pattern (TEXT)  e.g. 'P', 'P,S'
  aCL IgA (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 3
  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'MCTD, AMI', 'SLE'
  KCT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  RVVT (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  LAC (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '+'
  Symptoms (TEXT)  e.g. 'AMI', 'CNS lupus'
  Thrombosis (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 0
  FK: ID → Patient.ID

TABLE: Laboratory
  ID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (DATE) [PK, NOT NULL]
  GOT (INTEGER)  e.g. 34, 29
  GPT (INTEGER)  e.g. 36, 31
  LDH (INTEGER)  e.g. 567, 579

  T-CHO (INTEGER)  e.g. 165, 134

  CPK (INTEGER)  e.g. 9, 10

  WBC (REAL)  e.g. 5.0, 10.4

  HGB (REAL)  e.g. 6.4, 6.7
  HCT (REAL)  e.g. 20.3, 21.6

  APTT (INTEGER)  e.g. 108, 84
  FG (REAL)  e.g. 27.0, 38.6
  PIC (INTEGER)  e.g. 320, 513
  TAT (INTEGER)  e.g. 77, 108
  TAT2 (INTEGER)  e.g. 113, 88
  U-PRO (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '0'
  IGG (INTEGER)  e.g. 339, 771

  IGM (INTEGER)  e.g. 46, 132

  C4 (INTEGER)  e.g. 14, 26
  RNP (TEXT)  e.g. '0', '1'

  SSA (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  SSB (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  CENTROMEA (TEXT)  e.g. '0', 'negative'
  DNA (TEXT)  e.g. '41.9', '18'
  DNA-II (INTEGER)
  FK: ID → Patient.ID

TABLE: Patient
  ID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  SEX (TEXT)  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  Birthday (DATE)  e.g. '1934-02-13', '1937-05-02'
  Description (DATE)  e.g. '1994-02-14', '1996-12-01'
  First Date (DATE)  e.g. '1993-02-10', '1973-01-01'

  Diagnosis (TEXT)  e.g. 'RA susp.', 'PSS'

task prompt (3851 chars)
QUESTION: List the top nine districts, by descending order, from the highest to the lowest, the number of female account holders.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  A2 refers to districts; Female refers to gender = 'F'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many female employees are in charge of 3 or more territories?
  Hint: female employees refers to TitleOfCourtesy = 'Mrs.' or TitleOfCourtesy = 'Ms.'; in charge of 3 or more territories refers to TerritoryID > = 3;
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(EID) FROM ( SELECT T1.EmployeeID AS EID FROM Employees AS T1 INNER JOIN EmployeeTerritories AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID = T2.EmployeeID WHERE T1.TitleOfCourtesy IN ('Ms.' OR 'Mrs.') GROUP BY T1.EmployeeID HAVING COUNT(T2.TerritoryID) >= 3 ) T1

Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'
  FK: client_id → client.client_id
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'
  A5 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '80'
  A6 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '26'
  A7 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '6'
  A8 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  A9 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 5
  A10 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 100.0, 46.7
  A11 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 12541, 8507
  A12 (REAL)  e.g. 0.2, 1.6
  A13 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0.43, 1.85
  A14 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 167, 132
  A15 (INTEGER)  e.g. 85677, 2159
  A16 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 99107, 2674

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

```python
db.execute("SELECT type, COUNT(*) FROM disp GROUP BY type")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT type, COUNT(*) FROM disp GROUP BY type")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT d.A2, COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS female_account_holders
FROM client c
JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id
JOIN account a ON di.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE c.gender = 'F'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY female_account_holders DESC
LIMIT 9
""")
```

```python
db.sample_values("disp", "type")
```

task prompt (3794 chars)
QUESTION: Which are the top ten withdrawals (non-credit card) by district names for the month of January 1996?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Non-credit card withdraws refers to type = 'VYDAJ'; January 1996 can be found by date LIKE '1996-01%' in the database; A2 means district names

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: List out the name of the top 10 spenders and what credit card type are they using.
  Hint: top 10 spenders refers to MAX(PurchasePrice) LIMIT 10;
  SQL: SELECT T1.First, T1.Last, T2.CreditCardType FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN `transaction` AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID GROUP BY T1.CustomerID ORDER BY SUM(T2.PurchasePrice) DESC LIMIT 10

DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account

  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'

  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'

  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'

TABLE: disp

  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'

  A13 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0.43, 1.85
  A14 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 167, 132
  A15 (INTEGER)  e.g. 85677, 2159
  A16 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 99107, 2674

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals
FROM trans AS t
JOIN account AS a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district AS d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ'
  AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC
LIMIT 10;
""")
```

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals
FROM trans AS t

JOIN district AS d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ'
  AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC
LIMIT 10;
""")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT 1;")
```

task prompt (3517 chars)
QUESTION: Among the events attended by more than 10 members of the Student_Club, how many of them are meetings?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  meetings events refers to type = 'Meeting'; attended by more than 10 members refers to COUNT(event_id) > 10

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many of the students joined two organization?
  Hint: joined two organization refers to COUNT(organ) > = 2
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(name) FROM enlist WHERE organ >= 2

Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

TABLE: member
  member_id (TEXT) [PK]
  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'
  last_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Sanders', 'Gilmour'
  email (TEXT)  e.g. 'angela.sanders@lpu.edu', 'grant.gilmour@lpu.edu'
  position (TEXT)  e.g. 'Member', 'Inactive'
  t_shirt_size (TEXT)  e.g. 'Medium', 'X-Large'
  phone (TEXT)  e.g. '(651) 928-4507', '403-555-1310'
  zip (INTEGER)  e.g. 55108, 29440
  link_to_major (TEXT)  e.g. 'recxK3MHQFbR9J5uO', 'rec7BxKpjJ7bNph3O'
  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

TABLE: zip_code
  zip_code (INTEGER) [PK]
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Unique', 'Standard'
  city (TEXT)  e.g. 'Holtsville', 'Adjuntas'
  county (TEXT)  e.g. 'Suffolk County', 'Adjuntas Municipio'
  state (TEXT)  e.g. 'New York', 'Puerto Rico'
  short_state (TEXT)  e.g. 'NY', 'PR'

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(*) AS meeting_events_over_10
FROM (
  SELECT a.link_to_event
  FROM attendance AS a
  JOIN event AS e
    ON a.link_to_event = e.event_id
  GROUP BY a.link_to_event
  HAVING COUNT(a.link_to_event) > 10
     AND e.type = 'Meeting'
);
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT COUNT(*) AS meeting_events_over_10 FROM ( SELECT a.link_to_event FROM attendance AS a JOIN event AS e ON a.link_to_event = e.event_id GROUP BY a.link_to_event HAVING COUNT(a.link_to_event) > 10 AND e.type = 'Meeting' );")

task prompt (3757 chars)
QUESTION: What is the status of the event which bought "Post Cards, Posters" on 2019/8/20?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  'Post Cards, Posters' is an expense description; on 2019/8/20 refers to expense_date = '2019-8-20'; status of event refers to event_status

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the status of the orders placed on 04/10/2022?
  Hint: placed on 04/10/2022 refers to SUBSTR(order_date, 1, 10) = '2022-04-10'; status of order refers to status_value
  SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.status_value FROM order_status AS T1 INNER JOIN order_history AS T2 ON T1.status_id = T2.status_id INNER JOIN cust_order AS T3 ON T3.order_id = T2.order_id WHERE T3.order_date LIKE '2022-04-10%'

Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

TABLE: member
  member_id (TEXT) [PK]
  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'
  last_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Sanders', 'Gilmour'
  email (TEXT)  e.g. 'angela.sanders@lpu.edu', 'grant.gilmour@lpu.edu'
  position (TEXT)  e.g. 'Member', 'Inactive'
  t_shirt_size (TEXT)  e.g. 'Medium', 'X-Large'
  phone (TEXT)  e.g. '(651) 928-4507', '403-555-1310'
  zip (INTEGER)  e.g. 55108, 29440
  link_to_major (TEXT)  e.g. 'recxK3MHQFbR9J5uO', 'rec7BxKpjJ7bNph3O'
  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

TABLE: zip_code
  zip_code (INTEGER) [PK]
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Unique', 'Standard'
  city (TEXT)  e.g. 'Holtsville', 'Adjuntas'
  county (TEXT)  e.g. 'Suffolk County', 'Adjuntas Municipio'
  state (TEXT)  e.g. 'New York', 'Puerto Rico'
  short_state (TEXT)  e.g. 'NY', 'PR'

QUESTION: What are the budget category of the events located at MU 215 and a guest speaker type with a 0 budget spent?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  budget category refers to category; events located at refers to location; type = 'Guest Speaker'; 0 budget spent refers to spent = 0;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: For the all donations to the project 'Bringing Drama to Life', what is the percentage of the donation is paid by credit card?
  Hint: Bringing Drama to Life' is the title; Percentage = Divide(Count(payment_method = 'creditcard'), Count(projectid))*100;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.payment_method LIKE 'creditcard' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(donationid) FROM essays AS T1 INNER JOIN donations AS T2 ON T1.projectid = T2.projectid WHERE T1.title LIKE 'Bringing Drama to Life'

  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200

TABLE: major

TABLE: member

  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'

task prompt (3981 chars)

HINT (use these definitions exactly):

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Please list the credit card numbers of all the employees who have left the Finance Department.
  Hint: credit card number refers to CardNumber; employees who left the department refers to EndDate NOT null; Engineering Department is a name of department;
  SQL: SELECT T3.CardNumber FROM EmployeeDepartmentHistory AS T1 INNER JOIN Department AS T2 ON T1.DepartmentID = T2.DepartmentID INNER JOIN CreditCard AS T3 ON T1.ModifiedDate = T3.ModifiedDate INNER JOIN PersonCreditCard AS T4 ON T3.CreditCardID = T4.CreditCardID WHERE T2.Name = 'Finance' AND T1.EndDate IS NOT NULL

Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'

  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'

TABLE: loan

  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0

  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
